description: Alpha Factor Library - 30 factor definitions for equity long/short research

factors.name: 1-Month Price Momentum
factors.notes: Raw 1-month return. High turnover. Mean-reversion risk at short horizons.

factors.name: 3-Month Price Momentum
factors.notes: Intermediate momentum. More stable than 1M, less crowded than 12M.

factors.name: 6-Month Price Momentum (Jegadeesh-Titman)
factors.notes: Classic JT momentum. Best documented anomaly. Skip last month to avoid reversal.

factors.name: 12-Month Momentum (skip last month)
factors.notes: Skip last 1 month to avoid short-term reversal contamination. Standard in academic literature.

factors.name: 52-Week High Proximity
factors.notes: Anchoring-based factor. Stocks near 52w high have strong institutional attention. Range: [0,1].

factors.name: Earnings Momentum (SUE - Standardized Unexpected Earnings)
factors.notes: PEAD (post-earnings announcement drift). Requires earnings data. Normalize by historical forecast error std.

factors.name: Trailing P/E (inverted)
factors.notes: Use earnings yield (E/P) not P/E for linearity. Exclude negative earners or handle separately.

factors.name: Forward P/E (inverted)
factors.notes: NTM consensus. Forward earnings yield = 1/FwdPE. More forward-looking than trailing.

factors.name: Price-to-Book (inverted)
factors.notes: B/P ratio. Less effective post-2010 (intangibles issue). Still relevant for financials/industrials.

factors.name: EV/EBITDA (inverted)
factors.notes: EBITDA/EV yield. Capital structure neutral. Preferred over P/E for capital-intensive sectors.

factors.name: Free Cash Flow Yield
factors.notes: FCF = CFO - Capex. Harder to manipulate than earnings. Strong signal for mature companies.

factors.name: Dividend Yield
factors.notes: Blunt value signal. Exclude non-payers or treat separately. High yield can signal distress.

factors.name: Return on Equity
factors.notes: Decompose via DuPont: ROE = Net Margin * Asset Turnover * Leverage. Pure ROE conflates quality with leverage.

factors.name: Gross Margin Stability (3yr std, inverted)
factors.notes: Low GM volatility = pricing power + stable business model. Invert std so high = better. Min 8 quarters required.

factors.name: Debt/Equity (inverted)
factors.notes: Long low D/E (less levered). Sector-neutral ranking required (financials excluded or treated separately).

factors.name: Accruals Quality (Sloan)
factors.notes: Low accruals = earnings backed by cash. High accruals = potential earnings manipulation. Long low accruals.

factors.name: Earnings Consistency
factors.notes: Fraction of profitable quarters over 5 years. Range: [0,1]. Penalizes cyclical earnings.

factors.name: Piotroski F-Score
factors.notes: Score 0-9. Long F>=7, Short F<=2. Originally designed for value stocks. Combine with value factor for best results.

factors.name: Realized Volatility 60-Day (inverted)
factors.notes: Annualized realized vol. Low-vol anomaly: low-vol stocks outperform on risk-adjusted basis. Long low-vol.

factors.name: Idiosyncratic Volatility (CAPM residual, inverted)
factors.notes: Regress excess returns on market excess return. Take std of residuals. Idio vol puzzle: high ivol -> low returns.

factors.name: Beta to Market
factors.notes: Betting-against-beta (BAB) factor: low-beta stocks outperform on risk-adjusted basis per Frazzini-Pedersen.

factors.name: Maximum Drawdown 12-Month (inverted)
factors.notes: Captures tail risk not captured by vol. Long low-drawdown stocks. Correlated with RVOL but captures skewness.

factors.name: Downside Deviation (Sortino-style, inverted)
factors.notes: MAR = 0 (or risk-free rate). Only penalizes negative deviations unlike symmetric vol. Better for skewed distributions.

factors.name: Revenue Growth YoY
factors.notes: TTM vs prior TTM. Winsorize at 200% to handle base effects. Use 4-quarter sum for TTM calculation.

factors.name: EPS Growth YoY
factors.notes: Exclude when prior year EPS negative (undefined sign). Consider pairing with QUAL_ACCRUALS to filter quality.

factors.name: Revenue Acceleration
factors.notes: Second derivative of revenue. Acceleration = speeding up, not just growing fast. Key for growth-at-reasonable-price.

factors.name: Analyst Revision Breadth
factors.notes: Breadth of revisions, not magnitude. Captures consensus shift direction. Range: [-1, +1]. Strong short-term signal.

factors.name: Short Interest Ratio (inverted)
factors.notes: Short float %. High short = smart money bearish OR potential squeeze. Long low short (clean) as quality signal. Monitor squeeze risk separately.

factors.name: Analyst Consensus Rating
factors.notes: Convert: Strong Buy=5, Buy=4, Hold=3, Sell=2, Strong Sell=1. High consensus = priced in risk per S15/F15. Use with revision breadth.

factors.name: Insider Buying/Selling Ratio
factors.notes: Net insider buying ratio. Range: [-1, +1]. Exclude routine stock plan exercises (Rule 10b5-1). 6-month lookback. Strong signal for small/mid cap.

status_definitions.alive: IC > 0.02 consistently, IR > 0.3 over trailing 36 months
status_definitions.reversed: Factor signal inverted vs original hypothesis, IC consistently negative. Re-examine after 6 months.
status_definitions.dead: IC near zero (|IC| < 0.01), IR < 0.1 over trailing 36 months. Archive, do not use in models.
